title: I’m not sure who to tell

intro.description: You’ve got a concern or a complaint about someone — this is the chain for who to bring it to, especially if it involves a staff member.
standingRules: If the concern involves the person you’d normally report to, go one level up.
Concerns about an admin can also go straight to the CEO or through the Anonymous Report Form.

moderator.banner: Moderators: your one step is simple — bring the concern to a Coordinator or ping @Facilitators. The chain below is how staff take it from there.

q1.text: Is a young person at risk — safety, abuse, threats, self-harm?

q-grooming-staff.text: Does the grooming involve a staff member?

q3-grooming.note: Report to the level above whoever is involved. This never waits — and child-protection reporting is never delayed by the internal chain.

end-grooming-critical.headline: This is a critical incident, not a chain-of-command question.
steps: Continue in the Critical Incident Tool — it picks up at the grooming questions.

q2.text: Does the concern involve a staff member?

end-a.headline: This is a critical incident, not a chain-of-command question.
steps: Go to the Critical Incident tool now.
Loop in the Manager of Youth Programs and/or COO.

If the concern is about a staff member harming a youth: it’s still a critical incident first. Report to child protection per that tool, loop in the level above whoever’s involved, and never let the internal hierarchy delay the report.

moderator.headline: This is a critical incident — escalate now.
steps: Ping @Facilitators immediately with a link to the message or situation.
Don’t investigate and never screenshot imagery of a minor — note context only.
Stay available; a Facilitator takes it from here.

end-b.headline: Raise it in #flags-log.
steps: Post it as a flag in #flags-log — see the flags-log guide.
Keep it minimal, factual, and respectful; link the message rather than quoting sensitive detail.

moderator.headline: Bring it to the Facilitator team.
steps: Link the message in #mod-chat and ping @Facilitators.
Add one line of context — what you saw and when.

end-c.headline: Report to the Manager of Youth Programs.

moderator.headline: Moderators don’t climb this ladder.
steps: Bring it to a Coordinator or ping @Facilitators — they’ll take it up the chain.

end-d.headline: Report to the Chief Operations Officer.

moderator.headline: Moderators don’t climb this ladder.
steps: Bring it to a Coordinator or ping @Facilitators — they’ll take it up the chain.

moderator.headline: Moderators don’t climb this ladder.
steps: Bring it to a Coordinator or ping @Facilitators — they’ll take it up the chain.

end-f.headline: Report to the Chair of the Board.

moderator.headline: Moderators don’t climb this ladder.
steps: Bring it to a Coordinator or ping @Facilitators — they’ll take it up the chain.
